summary:
Israel conducted airstrikes on a residential zone and synagogue in Tehran after detecting Iranian missile launches. President Trump renewed threats against Iranian bridges and power plants ahead of a Tuesday deadline. This follows U.S. and Israeli strikes yesterday that killed more than 25 people including children in Iran. Iranian civilians report damage from the latest strikes.

In Gaza, an Israeli airstrike killed 10 people and wounded others near a school sheltering displaced Palestinians. The WHO halted evacuations in Gaza after the death of an aid worker. Israel permitted thousands of displaced Gazans to cross a bisecting military zone and return north after a hostage deadlock broke. Ukraine reported that Iranian missiles and drones struck U.S. bases days after Russian satellite surveys of those sites. NASA's four Artemis II astronauts surpassed Apollo 13's record for farthest distance from Earth during the Orion spacecraft's final lunar flyby phase.

NATO activated its first coordinated response after another deep-sea cable cut in the Baltic Sea, following suspected sabotage. Vietnam's National Assembly elected Communist Party chief To Lam, 68, as president after he consolidated rivals and oversaw province mergers; the government targets 10 percent annual growth. Taiwan Kuomintang Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun led a 14-member delegation on a six-day China visit, the first since 2016, and met Xi Jinping. U.S. Vice President Vance met Hungarian Prime Minister Orban and attended a campaign rally ahead of an election. Australian Federal Police arrested former soldier Ben Roberts-Smith, 47, a Victoria Cross recipient, on charges of murdering five unarmed prisoners in Afghanistan; this follows his lost defamation case against reporters.

headlines.title: Israel Strikes Tehran Residential Area and Synagogue as Trump Renews Threats to Iran
headlines.summary: Israel conducts airstrikes on Tehran residential zone and synagogue amid detected Iranian missile launches, while President Trump renews threats against Iranian bridges and power plants ahead of Tuesday deadline. Iranian civilians face damage; escalation threatens regional stability and global energy supplies.

framings.angle: Details Trump threats mixed messages and Israeli detection of missiles with Tehran residential airstrike
Reports US-Israeli projectile causing extensive damage to Tehran synagogue per Iranian video
Covers live updates on Pakistan peace efforts at critical stage and Israeli train warnings

headlines.title: NASA Artemis II Astronauts Break Apollo 13 Record for Farthest Distance from Earth
headlines.summary: NASA's four Artemis II astronauts surpass Apollo 13's record during Orion spacecraft's lunar flyby final phase. Enables unique human observations of moon regions. Boosts lunar science and paves way for future moon landings and Mars missions.

framings.angle: Highlights four astronauts breaking record for farthest human travel from Earth
Notes Orion in final phase breaking Apollo 13 distance record from Earth

headlines.title: Australia Arrests Ex-Soldier Ben Roberts-Smith on Five Afghan Murder Charges
headlines.summary: Australian Federal Police arrest Victoria Cross recipient Ben Roberts-Smith, 47, for allegedly murdering five unarmed prisoners in Afghanistan. Follows lost defamation case against reporters. Impacts military accountability and veteran status worldwide.

framings.angle: Names soldier as Ben Roberts-Smith linked to string of murders in probe
Links arrest to lost case against reporters alleging murders of unarmed Afghans

headlines.title: Israeli Airstrike Kills 10 Near Gaza School Shelter for Displaced Palestinians
headlines.summary: Israeli strike kills 10 and wounds others near school sheltering displaced people on Monday; WHO halts Gaza evacuations after aid worker death. Strains US-backed ceasefire, affecting civilians and humanitarian access in enclave.

headlines.title: Vietnam National Assembly Elects Party Chief To Lam as President
headlines.summary: Communist Party boss To Lam, 68, elected president after sweeping rivals and reforms merging provinces. Centralizes power in collective governance system. Targets 10 percent annual growth, shaping Vietnam's economic rise in Asia.

headlines.title: Taiwan KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun Starts China Visit Meeting Xi Jinping
headlines.summary: Kuomintang leader Cheng Li-wun leads 14-member delegation on six-day China trip, first since 2016, aiming for Taiwan Strait peace. Meets Xi amid rising tensions. Could ease cross-strait risks affecting global trade routes.

framings.angle: Notes opposition leader accepts Xi invitation as bridge for peace first visit since 2016
Describes journey of peace from Shanghai with planned Xi meeting drawing Taipei scrutiny

headlines.title: U.S. Vice President Vance Visits Hungary to Support Orban Before Election
headlines.summary: Vice President Vance meets PM Orban and attends campaign rally ahead of pivotal vote. Aims to bolster ally amid EU tensions. Influences NATO unity and European populist shifts.

headlines.title: Ukraine Says Iran Uses Russian Satellites to Target U.S. Military Bases
headlines.summary: Iranian missiles and drones hit U.S. bases days after Russian satellite surveys, per Ukrainian assessment. Exposes Russia-Iran military ties. Heightens risks in Middle East conflicts for allied forces.

headlines.title: NATO Deploys First Coordinated Response After Baltic Sea Cable Severance
headlines.summary: NATO activates response to suspected sabotage campaign following another deep-sea cable cut in Baltic Sea. Protects critical infrastructure. Signals alliance readiness against hybrid threats from Russia.
headlines.previousContext: Alliance mounts first response after second cable severed in suspected sabotage

headlines.title: Displaced Palestinians Return to Northern Gaza After Israel Opens Military Zone
headlines.summary: Israel permits Gazans to cross bisecting military zone post-hostage deadlock break. Thousands walk back north. Eases displacement crisis but risks renewed violence in divided enclave.
headlines.previousContext: Residents stream back on foot following breakthrough in hostage releases
